[IMAGE:0]It's getting on for midnight, so we can proceed with a new Magic Monday. Ask me anything about occultism and I'll do my best to answer it. Any question received by midnight Monday Eastern time will get an answer. (Any question received after then will not get an answer, and will likely just be deleted.) If you're in a hurry, or suspect you may be the 143,916th person to ask a question, please check out the very rough version 1.0 of The Magic Monday FAQ here.

The illustration? Another bit of Rosicrucian theory, this time having to do with the redirection of sexual energies into the upper centers of the subtle body. Max Heindel, whose system this describes, draws the usual distinction between the mystic and the occultist; in his view, the mystic path focuses on the heart and the occult path on the brain, but the energies activated by their work follow predictable patterns that eventually bring both to adeptship, as shown in the diagram. Heindel recommended celibacy for those who wanted to pursue either path; other Rosicrucian schools had other approaches. 

[IMAGE:1]

I've had several people ask about tipping me for answers here, and though I certainly don't require that I won't turn it down. You can use the button above to access my online tip jar. If you're interested in political and economic astrology, or simply prefer to use a subscription service to support your favorite authors, you can find my Patreon page here and my SubscribeStar page here. 
 
[IMAGE:2]I've also had quite a few people over the years ask me where they should buy my books, and here's the answer. Bookshop.org is an alternative online bookstore that supports local bookstores and authors, which a certain other gargantuan corporation doesn't, and I now have a shop there, which you can check out here. Please consider patronizing it if you'd like to purchase any of my books online.

And don't forget to look up your Pangalactic New Age Soul Signature at CosmicOom.com.

With that said, have at it!

Good evening Mr. Greer,

I want this incarnation to be one of my last. What can I do to ensure this is so?

Thank you in advance.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
There are two things you have to do to finish up your time on the human level. The first is to clear away your remaining karmic debts; the second is to finish the process of awakening on the mental plane. Neither of those is easy.

To clear away your karmic debts, first stop adding to them -- that means among other things behaving toward other people with fairness and compassion, no matter how they treat you. Then -- well, it depends on just how much karma you've got piled up and how much misery you're willing to endure in this life. You can, if you learn enough magic to do effective invocations of divine energies, call upon the forces of karma and ask them to give you the opportunity to clear things; just be aware that this may involve something on the order of dying slowly of an agonizing cancer, or being arrested and jailed for the next thirty years for a crime you didn't commit, or something else on that scale. It all depends on what you've got coming your way as a result of your actions in previous lives.

To finish awakening on the mental plane, systematic spiritual practice is essential. The basic daily regimen of a banishing ritual, 20-30 minutes of discursive meditation, and divination is a good start. If you feel called to ritual magic, any good system of magical training will give you plenty of rituals for spiritual development you can use to shift your inner maturation into high gear. If you feel called to mysticism instead, daily prayer will do the same thing; Evelyn Underhill's book Mysticism will give you some pointers. More generally, studying classic spiritual literature will help by giving you the conceptual frameworks you'll need to guide your practice.

replies.content: That's the smart way to do it -- concentrate on your spiritual development, do your best not to build up more noxious karma, and deal with whatever karmic burdens the universe hands you to resolve. comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
Good evening JMG!

I've been thinking about silence and one of the areas that it clashes with my upbringing is the somewhat simplistic notion of you should never lie. I actually think lying can be very useful sometimes, for example when one is trying to conceal something or when trying to dodge unwanted attention but I am facing resistance with this. According to you, when is it wrong to lie? And how can I deprogram myself that it is always wrong?

I had a fairly traumatic high school experience with abuse, and through journaling I realized that I've become aggressive in some areas of my life as a way of dealing with the shame. It turns out that is a thing, men become aggressive when you make them feel weak. I no longer want to feel anger about this, and I've found that the primary feelings are shame from being publicly ridiculed and frustration with being powerless about this. I remember you say that part of the healing is by allowing yourself to feel those but I think I am stuck. How can I even begin to forgive people that did me so much harm and make the anger go away?

Lastly, can the consequences of my actions that I need to face that start to pile up in front of me do to magic practice manifest as repeated diseases instead of events? I've had quite a few weeks of abnormally high injuries and infections.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) Lying is wrong when the person you're lying to has a valid reason to know the truth that you're concealing from them. If there's no good reason for someone to know something, concealing it from them is morally neutral, and if the truth will hurt someone, hiding it from them can be morally positive.

2) Forgiveness is overrated. The challenge you face is in letting go of the burden of pain they inflicted on you, so you can go on to live your life despite them. Don't try to make the anger go away; accept that you feel angry about what was done to you, and that you don't have to let the anger run your life. Some people find that journaling helps; some people find that joining a martial arts school and pounding the bejesus out of a heavy bag helps. Massage nearly always helps. It's a long rough road to outgrow something like that -- trust me, I know. Take it a step at a time.

3) Yes, if that's the form your karma takes. Be patient with it and keep up with your practices.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Thank you. That is really good advice, I'll get pounding!

This is quite a ride isn't it? I knew magic was going to be different, but it is quite another thing to see it happening. After a year of fumbling and pushing myself I've actually started to enjoy the time I spend with my practices. No other thing gives me the same stability and vitality as my daily banishing and invoking. Heck it's been ages since I've felt like this. I'm actually... enthusiastic about something. And this is just the beginning!

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: I know the feeling. Welcome to the Path!

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Re Answer Number 2 -
What exactly is forgiveness? I was raised Catholic, so I remember that forgiveness was a major deal in that religion, but I realize that I can’t really define it. Hearing you say that forgiveness is overrated is somewhat shocking to me, but it also raises my curiosity.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Forgiveness is the process of letting go of all hard feelings toward someone who has harmed you. There are times when it's a good thing, but in those cases it happens naturally. I've seen vast amounts of half-conscious hypocrisy on the part of people who think they ought to forgive and just bottle up their hard feelings, and I've also seen vast amounts of manipulation by people and institutions who deliberately hurt people and then demand that their victims ought to forgive them and hold no hard feelings -- usually so that the victimizers can abuse them again. Thus my comment that forgiveness is overrated.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
You pretty much never have to lie, if you don't want to. I think about this a lot, lately, for some strange reason (example below). I get breathe-into-a-paper-bag afraid of having to lie because the problem is that unless you remember your whole story right, eventually, you'll trip up. I say wrong things all the time, because of faulty memory, or other things, but never consciously. Because my memory isn't good enough!

My husband has a slightly different approach, whereby he does make something up, but not directly opposed to the question (like, he says blue instead of no when the answer is yes), and so outrageously obviously a lie, that is it even a lie if your don't expect someone to believe you?

Example:

"Did you get your vaccine yet?"

Me: "I still haven't received my appointment time for my second shot".

Didn't say whether I'd gotten my first, did I? Or whether I'd even tried to make an appointment. Every word is true. If someone is asking because they will only feel safe around a fully vaccinated person, they still get the information they need; I am not "totally protected".

My husband: "All three, from that guy set up in the park. I was wiped for days, and my arm is still sore from the belt". This takes a very particular personality and charisma to pull off - if it isn't a situation where the goal is changing the subject entirely, he'll use my technique.

Obviously, you can tell as much of the truth as you want to someone you wouldn't have considered lying to in the first place (someone who would have accepted silence as an answer). If, for some reason, the person is instead being extremely pointed and specific, it's probably a given they don't need to know, so "It's not your business one way or the other" is also the whole truth.

comments.content:
JMG,

1. Is it possible to perform too much banishing by repeating the LBRP too often over a period of time without any corresponding invocation to balance it out? What would be the psychological state that would result, and how should one get out of it?

2. I’m looking for a ritual in the GD tradition to improve focus and memory for work purposes. Due to the nature of my job, I need to be able to stare at a screen and concentrate on some fairly boring material over an extended period of time while being detail-oriented and rigorous. The ritual would ideally be one that I could combine with the LBRP and perform on a regular basis, say, prior to starting work. Do you have any suggestions? Perhaps a GIRH of Mercury followed by an affirmation to that effect, while burning some white sandalwood?

3. What is a polite way to break off a relationship with a deity? Is such a thing a good idea in general?

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) Yes, which is why you should take up the Middle Pillar exercise within a few months of learning the LBRP. The Middle Pillar is a good robust invocation and balances the banishing aspect. Too much banishing without invocation, if the banishing is the Hermetic GD version of the LBRP, will tend to make you pale, low-energy, and too detached from the material world. Fortunately the Middle Pillar will fix that promptly.

2) The classic GD way to do this would be to work up as quickly as possible to making and consecrating a talisman of Mercury -- that's far more effective than doing a ritual every day. If you can do hexagram rituals with effect, and know how to open and close a temple using the Watchtower ritual or some similar method, you already know what you need to know to consecrate a talisman, and that's a good piece of training too.

3) Sure. It happens all the time. Burn some incense, thank the deity for the help he or she has given you, explain that you feel called to work with another deity now, and finish with another prayer of thanks. That's polite, and has good results.

comments.content:
JMG,

I'd hate to dump a wall of text on you this week, but I finally had a true, proper, "what the hell just happened?" moment this weekend. I have a theory, but wanted to see if it checks out with you.

On Friday night as I was drifting off I was startled awake by a strong growl from above me. I’m pretty used to hypnagogia etc, but this felt more, say, external. I did a shorthand LBRP laying in bed to clear my mind and went back to sleep. The next morning I woke up and stumbled out of bed naked as usual to find myself standing in my doorway quite exposed. Amusingly, the door was wide open and my roommates were right around the corner. I’m extremely keen on my privacy, so my door is always closed and locked. Granted the knob isn’t amazing, but I’ve never had it just pop open like that in the five months I’ve been here.

The next day during my practice I had a very strong intrusive thought right towards the end of my LBRP. Calling me by name, it said something like “Hey Reid, don’t you know there are spirits ALL around you ALWAYS?”. I was creeped out but tried to carry on. After I finished my MP I sat down to do my daily discursive. It was going pretty well until I realized that there were two different voices distinct from “me” dialoging with each other about the topic in my head, as opposed to my normal one track ruminations. Again, I was pretty creeped out but did my best to keep going. Pretty quickly after I felt this very strong thick presence pass through my body from the back to the front. The atmosphere was palpable enough that I internally asked “Is there something else here right now?”, but I didn’t get an answer. I had a strange ringing in my ear too. I eventually got too uneasy and had to stop my meditation early, and I haven’t prematurely ended a mediation since probably December.

After that, I did the closing gesture and immediately went to my magic journal to write the entry for the day. I don’t keep my phone in the circle when i’m practicing and I set it on my bed when I use the stopwatch to time my rituals, but guess what was suddenly acting up and wouldn’t let me see the times.

As I was writing I remembered the two strange events the night before and the morning. After I finished my the entry I did a another quick LBRP just to clear the air again, and then, in true Magic Monday fashion, I immediately went and got the vinegar and a bowl, haha.

So after quite a bit of reflection I have to ask: 1. Do you think that this just the Watcher suddenly getting very fancy and tricking me into scaring myself?

I have a few reservations about this explanation but I reasoned this possibility out from the fact that A) I don’t think I did anything out of the ordinary that would dredge up weird spirits on Friday, B) it obviously knew my still remaining materialist hangups, and since I banished I don’t think anything other than me would be hanging around, C) I mentioned this in my comment last week, but I’ve been getting a lot of internal and external pushback (sudden depression, apathy, unexpected errands, etc) and reasons I shouldn’t do my practices, and it would seem natural that the complexities would increase I think.

The reservations I have are mostly about the amount of the weird things that happened and the intensity of them. I don’t mess around with it much at the moment, but my experiences with proper invocation haven’t been anything remotely close to this. The door being open is also tripping me up, but I guess that's assuming is all assuming that and the stuff on Friday night isn’t entirely unrelated.

A few additional questions:

2. Is there anything I could have done to have handled this better?

3. Would divination be appropriate way to get insight into what happened? Do you have any pointers on what a tarot spread for that might look like?

Thanks a million,
Reid

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) This is what's known as astral static. It's a more colorful example than usual, so yes, the Watcher may well be involved.

replies.title: Re: Initiation, Shrines and a Seal
replies.content:
1) Not true at all. I've had the experience of self-initiating in some traditions, being formally initiated in others, and in a couple of cases, self-initiating and then receiving the formal initiation. If anything, self-initiation -- if you do it with the necessary discipline and persistence -- produces a deeper effect and greater power than your average initiatory ceremony can manage. Best of all -- and this is the approach that many Western occult schools use -- is to combine the two, and have the student do self-initiatory practices as preparation and follow-up for the ritual.

3) I don't know -- none of the traditions in which I've worked do anything with spirit animals.

comments.title: Alchemical Symbols of Planets
comments.content:
On the "High Magic" blog, dennisg had posted about his deck (The Alchemical Tarot of Marseille by Robert M Place, pictures here: https://robertmplacetarot.com/the-alchemical-tarot-of-marseilles/). The symbol for Mercury on The Magician card has a dot in the circle, which made me think of the Sun symbol. The horns are a crescent Moon, so it was easy to see the Sun, the Moon, and the cross from the Earth symbol in his depiction.

I thought this related to the Sphinx's riddle. In Paths of Wisdom, you explain the Supernal Triad and I thought the Moon (as feminine) could represent the Receptive Form, the Sun (as masculine) could represent the Creative Force, and then the cross would represent the material. But these symbols do not align with the symbols for Binah and Chokmah.

And, if you see Mercury's symbol as Moon/Sun/Earth, it is easy to see Venus' symbol as Sun/Earth. Previously, I had done some reading on Satan (to better understand some of the Tree of Knowledge references in the introduction) and was surprised to learn that Venus (the Morning Star) was at one time considered Lucifer ("light bringer"), because it was seen preceding the dawn (the arrival of the Sun).

So the symbol of Venus is a duality and then the Mercury symbol adds the crescent moon, another symbol of duality. I pondered and pondered and could not come up with a way to combine the dualities in a way that resulted in something that made sense in terms of the Sphinx's riddle. I talked about it to my husband last night (who is not participating in the Levi book club, but trying to explain things to him helps me clarify ideas in my head, plus he often has a different perspective, which sends me careening down a new tangent… but more on that in a separate submission).

This morning, I researched the alchemical symbols of planets, to see if that would provide me any insights. I learned that the Venus symbol originally was based on the Greek letter Phi (the first letter of the Greek name of the planet, "Latinized" (if that is a word) to Phosphoros). Much later, the horizontal line (to make a cross) was added to Christianize the symbol. (And apparently the Christians did that to other planet symbols, too.)

That completely obliterates my idea of the Venus symbol being a duality of Sun/Earth (elements/material). Unless the early occultists added the horizontal line so it *would* better represent a duality and let the rest of the world *think* it was to Christianize it, because the occultists are sneaky that way. But I have not been able to prove or disprove that with any sources that I can find.

Are you willing to provide an explanation of what the planets' symbols mean (I'm mostly interested in Mercury, Venus, and Earth) or is there a resource you can recommend that does or is this one of those things I just get to meditate on and come up with my own explanation that works for me and it doesn't matter what the originators of the symbols meant?

As always, thank you!

replies.title: Re: Alchemical Symbols of Planets
replies.content:
Ignore the "official" account; it's most likely some academic's brain fart. The emblems of the planets have been interpreted symbolically as combinations of the solar circle, the lunar crescent, and the cross of the elements since the early Renaissance if not before. John Dee's Monas Hieroglyphica is one source; almost every work of magical philosophy in the French tradition from Papus to the present day discusses this, as does Regardie's The Golden Dawn.

According to the French occult scheme, the lunar crescent represents receptiveness toward the spiritual; the solar disk represents vital energy; and the cross is the world of elemental matter. The sign of Venus thus raises vital force above matter -- thus its connection with beauty and love -- while Saturn raises the cross above the crescent -- thus its connection with the kind of hard material conditions where the voice of the spirit is very difficult to hear. You can work out the others yourself in meditation.

replies.title: Re: Alchemical Symbols of Planets
replies.content: Thank you!!!

comments.title: An Answer for the Sphinx
comments.content:
*post at your discretion*

My husband considers himself a Bard, as in lore master. He doesn't think he can do magic, because he doesn't feel 'tingles' like I do and, as of now, I have not been able to convince him that magic isn't about the tingles. My not-so-subtle efforts to assist him on the Path involve me asking him questions about lore that are referenced in my readings (which also assist me on my Path, plus I've decided that the conversations are a manifestation of ideas onto Malkuth, which is hopefully assisting Gaia). I had not previously asked him about the Sphinx riddle, because I've come up with answers that seemed plausible and have helped me further my understanding about other things (and I have plenty of other things to ask/talk about).

Last night, though, I asked him about planet symbols, etc., because I am stymied with the Mercury symbol. I explained that I was trying to figure out how to relate it to the Sphinx riddle (as referenced in my earlier submission). He didn't know about planet symbols and hadn't thought much about the Sphinx riddle; I explained as much as I could until I got tired, then I fell asleep.

He, though, did not fall asleep. He pondered and pondered and eventually got up to look up the actual wording of the Sphinx riddle and had an epiphany. He typed it up as an email, to get the details down before he went to sleep and sent it to me. I read it this morning and we talked for another hour or so… he had more ideas to incorporate than he had included in his email and I had not previously explained to him about how learning to control our animals/passions/elements are part of enlightenment.

His answer to the Sphinx blew my mind. I wanted to share with you. If you think it would be valuable to the Commentariot, feel free to post (I asked/received his permission to share). I have not edited (except for some asterisks for what might be inappropriate vocabulary on your blog) what he sent me, which is pasted below.

**********

When humans train a horse to be ridden. There are three primary gates: Walk, Trot, Canter/Gallop.

Walk is pretty plain often 4 feet are on the ground 4 legs are in constant use many rhythms are found amongst all horse type creatures but it’s a 4 beat gait.

Trot is a two gait beat where the diagonal pairs of legs fall together.

While the Gallop is just the horse hauling a**. The Canter is a specialized training that tempers a horse from an all-out Gallop. This maintains control of the animal by a rider lengthening is ability to move man and equipment quickly but not recklessly. You guessed it the Canter it’s a 3 beat gait. 4, 2, 3.

Historically the canter and the gallop were considered the same just at differing speeds. But this was later disproven by use of staged photography. It proved the Canter has three feet on the ground in its locomotion. The Gallop is so fast it requires a horse to have all four feet in the air during its locomotion.

It has always been three natural gaits for horse types with the Canter being considered a Gallop, as one in the same. However, many ancient horsemen (especially military) knew but couldn’t actually prove the Three Legged Gait. Interestingly enough there was a man…

Xenophon Student of Socrates developed the training of the Canter as an advanced gait of a three beat step where one foot pushes off while three feet land and prop the horse to a pivot. A Cantering horse moves on three feet. He wrote one of the earliest books “On Horsemanship” (355 BC) It was well known and popular. But Xenophon attributed his insight to Simon of Athens who lived and wrote about horsemanship. (circa 460 BC) Oedipus The King was written around 430 BC. So it would certainly be possible that a riddle from a learned, experience horseman would know of a 4th gait (the Canter)

Even today a horse must learn to Canter and is trained to Walk first, Trot Second, and Canter Last. The gallop is just hauling ass and for a brief moment suspended in air.

The riddle of the Sphinx in its long form:

A thing there is whose voice is one; (Man and beast working as one)
Whose feet are four and two and three. (4 walk, 2 Trot, 3 Canter)
So mutable a thing is none (Ask a horseman about this bond, when horses were absolutely everthing)
That moves in earth or sky or sea. (A straightforward description about differing terrain, but remember a Gallop has no feet on the ground and “so the horse can jump”
When on most feet this thing doth go, (Either a Walk or Gallop both are 4 feet gaits. Basically using most of the feet but my guess is Walk)
Its strength is weakest and its pace most slow (Walk is slowest pace and the does not generate the inertia to power through. An attack, plowing, water resistance.)

Sophicles invented a riddle or heard a riddle or formed a riddle based on that which came from a Cavalry Officers (Simon of Athens) writings on a specialized subject to which only a few would foster a guess towards; even if they had some limited knowledge or speculation. That riddle defined as: A question or statement intentionally phrased so as to require ingenuity in ascertaining its answer or meaning, typically presented as a game. So…

A man could be correct in part. (as you spoke of last night)
But a horse would not be correct. It is not trained or being ridden in all circumstance of the riddle.
However, a man riding a horse, or a Horseman would answer the riddle and I bet you anything, even a weak bard could sell it.

There is something called a card press that you may be able to find in an antique store. One of my husband's friends has one. If you've ever seen a book press, it's a card deck sized version of that. I don't know if you could wrap the cards in silk or linen while you are pressing them, but you can put them in a card press if they are not wrapped. comments.title: Psychic Attack
comments.content:
Hello JMG,

Questions re psychic attack:

I live in an apartment, and I've been having increasing problems with the upstairs neighbor, an older male in his 60's. He seems to have a lot of problems, is single, unemployed, has a drug history and struggles with some type of mental illness. I'm a single male in my 40's. Problems started when I was laid off from my job 6+ months ago, so I'm at home a lot more. At first, the neighbor started making excessive noise; then using noise to sort of comment on what I was doing or even thinking. It seems like he can sense where I am in my apartment, sometimes what I'm doing and even what I'm thinking about or reading. He then used noises to "comment" on that. He would also follow me from above and stand over me, it's really weird. I used to meditate daily, but I stopped because whenever I meditated, he would walk over and stand directly above me; it felt like he was sucking up the energy. When I stopped meditating, he seemed to get angry. He started to stomp over my bed at night, waking me up from sleep. I started to suffer sleep deprivation, and slept in my car or at my friends' house for a month.

It sounds like paranoid schizophrenia, doesn't it. But I talked to my doctor, a nurse, family and friends to make sure I wasn't going insane. I also observed myself and the situation for many months. I don't think I'm crazy. But I do feel like I have had a unique window into how it is possible to use psychic / occult powers to harass someone to the extent that they end up in a state of mental illness or even suicide. For myself, however, I don't think that is the danger. I am more worried that I will end up in jail for physically beating the black magic out of this dude. -- not joking, actually :(

Anyway -- I documented these instances, made several formal complaints to property management, and moved to a new apartment in the same complex. I thought my problems would be over, but instead the attacks have continued.

I would like to describe to you a typical example:
Two nights ago, I was asleep in my bed, it was about 5:30am. The quality of the sleep becomes -- different somehow. It's feels like a very noisy dream, like opening a door into Grand Central Station or something, with a cacophony of loud voices, noises and sounds all going off at once. Like a very loud dream. And then I wake up with a jolt. The jolt feels like I just got hit with a big electric shock -- my body energy feels all weird and quivery, like I'm not in alignment -- my mind is instantly awake but also stunned. It feels like a violent electric punch or shock -- I wonder if this is what a heart attack or stroke feels like.

When this used to happen in my former apartment, I could hear the neighbor's steps: he was directly above my bed, and then would move to the other end of the apartment, and presumably go back to his own bed. In my new apartment, I can't hear the footsteps anymore, but the feeling is the same: getting forcibly woken up by some kind of energy blast that messes up my energy, and I can't get back to sleep.

I do not have magical training, I guess I'm more mystically inclined, but I'm learning about magic. So, to protect myself, I have tried many, many things (hoodoo baths, red bag amulet, vinegar bowl, banishing ritual of the cross, praying to the deities & angels I connect with, protection crystals, reading books on the subject, etc etc etc). Everything helps a little, but -- the attacks are still happening, so -- I am temporarily sleeping at a friend's house again. Moving again is a painful idea, because it's almost impossible to find another affordable rental in this super-expensive town.

Questions:
-- Would you agree that this is psychic attack?
-- How dangerous are these attacks to my physical health? How worried should I be about this? Like, is this going to kill me?
-- Where should I go for help? Do you think I should see a psychic about this?
Or should I just move out?
-- Do you have any advice for me?

Thank you for Magic Mondays, I am a long-time reader, but don't comment much. Thank you so much for your time.

-- Anonymous

replies.title: Re: Psychic Attack
replies.content: It's hard for me to tell at a distance. I will say that this really does sound like paranoid schizophrenia -- the fact that the attacks keep happening despite magical protections is a good indication that the issue is internal to you, not something that someone else is doing. I'd encourage you to consult, not a doctor or nurse or family or friends, but a qualified psychiatrist who can help you sort this out.

comments.title: Unicursal Hexagrams, Pronunciation
comments.content:
Dear J.M. Greer

1) Israel Regardie says that for things like the Ritual of the Hexagram, 'use the unicursal hexagram only,' as 'the other forms are superfluous.' What are your thoughts on the matter ?

2) This might be a silly question, but what is the proper way to pronounce or say aloud the paired grade numbers of Golden Dawn-style grade systems, such as '0 = 0,' '1=10,' etc. ? As an autodidact who has relied on books rather than living teachers, I've committed some pronunciation howlers, so I now try to avoid such simple errors when I can.
Additionally, one variant I've seen used by the Ciceros and the A∴A∴ has the first number with a superscript zero or degree symbol, and the second with a superscript square, 0^o = 0^□, 1^o = 10^□, etc. I've seen it printed in Israel Regardie's books with the large square next to the numeral, thus 4^0 = 7□ ;
Do you know the significance and pronunciation of these ?

Thank you very much for this forum
Regards,
Josh Rout

replies.title: Re: Unicursal Hexagrams, Pronunciation
replies.content:
1) In my experience Regardie was wrong. The regular forms have a different and more balanced energy.

2) The circle and square are original, and come from the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn. The first number, with the circle, gives the order of the grades; the second, with the square, is the number of the Sephirah to which it is assigned. As for pronunciation, everyone I've ever heard says "One equals Ten," or what have you.

comments.title: Whispers From Antiquity
comments.content:
Hello Mr. Greer,

You recently wrote an article on your main blog discussing the possibility of advanced technology in ancient civilizations. You wrote the following in that article,

"In the particular schools in which I had my training, the idea was that ours is the fifth cycle of hominid civilizations on Earth. The four previous cycles are called the Polarian, Hyperborean, Lemurian, and Atlantean ages... If the occult teachings I learned are correct, this isn’t the end of the present cycle of civilizations—there are supposed to be several more great civilizations before the current cycle comes to an end—but we’re past the peak, and what remains of our civilization and the two to come will go through their own shorter cycles in the course of a greater decline. After that? There are supposed to be two more cycles of civilization, separated from ours and each other by long intervals of tribal existence, before humanity finishes its time on this planet."

This fascinates me on a whole series of levels, so I'll try to limit my questions so that you can reasonably answer them.

1) Is there a particular source that outlines this vision of the future? I want to learn more.
2) What counts as a civilization in this context? Are you counting the beginning of our civilization as the industrial revolution, the first beginnings of western civilization in ancient Greece, the dawn of agriculture, or what? I wasn't sure, so I also wasn't sure how far these civilizations were expected to last into the future.
3) And along that line of thought, this sounds like a vision for the future measured by thousands or maybe tens of thousands of years. The vision of the future you gave in "The Next 10 Billion Years" sounded like you thought our species might last millions of years. Have I misunderstood your occult teacher's timeline or do you reject their teachings on this point?

replies.title: Re: Whispers From Antiquity
replies.content:
1) I got it from bits and scraps in various pieces of Theosophical and occult literature.

2) I define a civilization in terms derived from Spengler. Western civilization -- what he called the Faustian culture -- was born in Europe around 1000.

3) Um, "The Next Ten Billion Years" was a work of fiction, you know. If the occult teachings are correct -- and of course that's anybody's guess -- we probably have a million years or so left, since each cycle of civilizations is separated by long periods in which humanity exists as scattered tribal societies.

"Hail to thee, Everliving Sunna, whose
Well-seen token rises now in the heavens!

I am planning on working out some kennings/heiti for Sunna to use for each step of the salutation, but I wanted to get the physical movements right and build the habit of doing it morning, midday, and evening first, so I haven't gotten there yet.

comments.title: shaman
comments.content:
I have a friend who was raised in a non-western culture by a magic user relative. That relative was well known and sought out to the bane of the medical establishment and Christian leadership, but a lot of them secretly sought out their help as well. My friend told me some amazing stories about them, and I pointed out that he is also using magic in his career per the definition of Dion Fortune.

It seems that something got passed down to him, and although it had a profound impact on his life apparently it was working only on a semi-conscious level. After mentioning it to him he thought about it for a bit and realized it was true, and came out with some strange and wonderful examples I had no idea about.

When he asked me about how it was possible that something might get passed down I admitted that I have no idea, but most people have to work really hard at it to get anywhere.

I would like to encourage him to find out more, partly because I want to know more myself. Actually the reason that I initially explored Druidry was because after spending time with his community I had been exposed to hidden things that anthropologists and missionaries prefer to ignore, and it made me wonder what was hidden in my own culture.

How does this stuff get passed down, and what would be a good place for him to start looking since his relative is no longer living?

replies.title: Re: shaman
replies.content: It's entirely possible for a qualified initiate to pass on an energetic connection to a tradition without saying a word. Such methods as the laying on of hands are among the options here. As for going further, well, presumably your friend's relative was not the only such practitioner in that society, and finding another practitioner who is willing to teach is the usual method. That said, since you haven't mentioned what culture we're talking about, I have no way of knowing what that culture's traditions might involve.
